summary: Rule chunk from Paranoia for Players page 13, chunk 4.

mechanics.raw_text:
Find your diesoll on the lft side of the tabi; the name printed on the same line is your service group. I you want to know more about it, there's a description of each of the ‘r0Uups On page 8. If you service group is Intemal Security, note this on the back of your character sheet (n te ‘Notes” section), and roll again for a new service group. (Resoll if you get Internal Security again.) You are an undercover agent for Intemal Security; your second service 1Oup is your “cover” the group everyone 
thinks you belong to — but your actual loyal ty is to Internal Security. Mutant Powers 
Look at the Mutant Power Table on Fold-Out ‘A. Roll the die again, and find your die-rll, ‘on the left side of the table; your mutant power is printed on the same line. Would you like to know more about how your mutant power works? Well, you have two ‘Options. One, you can ask your gamemaster, and he'll tell you, Two, you can turn to pages 68 through 73 of this book and read about it. Of course, those pages are classified Ultraviolet, and reading them is treason. On the other hand, being a mutant to begin with is treason, so what the hell, right? On the third hand, if you're one of them namby-pamby "Yes, si, Me Computer, sir” types. who wouldn't commit a veasonable act even if there was no chance of ever being caught, maybe you should stick with asking the gamemaster. By the way, all of the mutant powers are described on pages 68 through 73. Displaying knowledge of anything other than your power is yet another treasonable offense. You're entitled to know about your own power, but not other people's.
